title: The Orders & Orderlines "states" 🗃
bodyHtml:
This article is a bit technical and particularly useful for Sellers choosing a Technical connection (such as API or FTP). You can also find more information here: https://doc.backmarket.fr/#taxonomy 

 

From its creation to the moment it's totally processed, an order passes by several states. 

Order states

When an order is created, it's in state 0. The payment validation is pending. Back Market is doing verifications to check the customer identity. Orders in this state must not be shipped.

If the payment process fails, the order passes to state 8. You must ignore this order. Actually, it should never arrive in your system, as we filter them out when sending you the order list.

If the payment is checked and validated, the order passes to state 1. You must process the orderlines.

When all the orderlines are managed (accepted or cancelled), the order passes to state 3. You must ship the accepted orderlines and refund the cancelled ones.

When all the orderlines are shipped or refunded, the order passes to state 9: the order is processed.
 

Orderlines states

When an order is created, all its orderlines are in state 0. You have to wait for payment confirmation.

If the payment process fails, all the orderlines pass to state 7.

When the order passes to state 1, all its orderlines pass to state 1. You have received the payment, and you must now validate or cancel each orderline.

If you cancel an orderline, it passes to state 4. The customer will be refunded for this orderline.

If you accept an orderline, it passes to state 2. You must now prepare the corresponding product for shipment.

When all the orderlines are in state 2 or 4, the order passes to state 3.

Even if you accepted an orderline, you can cancel it afterward. If you or the customer cancel it after it has been validated, the orderline passes from state 2 to state 5. The customer will be refunded for this orderline.

Once you have shipped the orderline, you need to update the orderline from the state 2 to the state 3. 

Note that, since all orderlines are gathered in the same package, you don’t need to update every orderlines to the state 3 but just once for the whole order. Indeed, every orderline of this order will be set to this state automatically.

Finally, if the customer makes a refund request after shipping, the orderline passes from state 3 to state 6. The customer will be refunded for this orderline.
